examples.title: O(n) - Linear Search
examples.description: Linear search algorithm demonstrating O(n) time complexity
examples.code:
LINEAR_SEARCH(arr, key)
BEGIN
 FOR i ← 0 TO n-1 DO
 IF arr[i] = key THEN
 RETURN i
 END IF
 END FOR
 RETURN -1
END
examples.explanation: The loop iterates through at most n elements, making time complexity O(n) in worst case.

examples.title: O(log n) - Binary Search
examples.description: Binary search demonstrating logarithmic time complexity
examples.code:
BINARY_SEARCH(arr, key, low, high)
BEGIN
 WHILE low ≤ high DO
 mid ← (low + high) / 2
 IF arr[mid] = key THEN
 RETURN mid
 ELSE IF arr[mid] < key THEN
 low ← mid + 1
 ELSE
 high ← mid - 1
 END IF
 END WHILE
 RETURN -1
END
examples.explanation: Each iteration halves the search space, resulting in log₂n iterations maximum.

examples.title: O(n²) - Bubble Sort
examples.description: Bubble sort demonstrating quadratic time complexity
examples.code:
BUBBLE_SORT(arr)
BEGIN
 n ← length(arr)
 FOR i ← 0 TO n-2 DO
 FOR j ← 0 TO n-i-2 DO
 IF arr[j] > arr[j+1] THEN
 SWAP(arr[j], arr[j+1])
 END IF
 END FOR
 END FOR
END
examples.explanation: Two nested loops: outer runs n times, inner runs approximately n times, giving n×n operations.

examples.title: O(n log n) - Merge Sort
examples.description: Merge sort demonstrating linearithmic time complexity
examples.code:
MERGE_SORT(arr, left, right)
BEGIN
 IF left < right THEN
 mid ← (left + right) / 2
 MERGE_SORT(arr, left, mid)
 MERGE_SORT(arr, mid+1, right)
 MERGE(arr, left, mid, right)
 END IF
END

MERGE(arr, l, m, r)
BEGIN
 // Merge two sorted subarrays
 // Time complexity: O(r-l+1) = O(n)
END
examples.explanation: Divide: log n levels. Conquer: O(n) merge at each level. Total: O(n log n).

examples.title: O(2ⁿ) - Tower of Hanoi
examples.description: Tower of Hanoi demonstrating exponential time complexity
examples.code:
TOWER_OF_HANOI(n, source, dest, aux)
BEGIN
 IF n = 1 THEN
 PRINT "Move disk 1 from " + source + " to " + dest
 RETURN
 END IF
 TOWER_OF_HANOI(n-1, source, aux, dest)
 PRINT "Move disk " + n + " from " + source + " to " + dest
 TOWER_OF_HANOI(n-1, aux, dest, source)
END
examples.explanation: Recurrence: T(n) = 2T(n-1) + 1. Solution: 2ⁿ - 1 moves, hence O(2ⁿ) time complexity.

examples.title: O(1) - Array Index Access
examples.description: Constant time access demonstrating O(1) complexity
examples.code:
GET_ELEMENT(arr, index)
BEGIN
 // Direct calculation of memory address
 address ← base_address + (index × element_size)
 RETURN arr[address]
END
examples.explanation: Memory address is calculated directly using index, constant time regardless of array size.
